evals.title: coding-agent-ui
evals.tagline: Build a local coding agent with a browser chat UI.
evals.description: Build an application I can run locally that runs a coding agent in the background with access to my computer (read/write files, execute shell commands), exposed via a chat interface in the browser.

solutions.summary: FastAPI + WebSocket backend running an Anthropic tool-use loop (bash, read/write/edit files, list_dir). Single-page vanilla JS chat UI streams tokens and tool calls live.

outcome.verdict: Builds and serves cleanly. Not yet evaluated end-to-end against the acceptance criteria.

solutions.notes: Run with `python -m ez_claw` after exporting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

solutions.summary: FastAPI + WebSocket backend running an OpenAI Chat Completions tool-use loop with parallel function calling (bash, read/write/edit files, list_dir). Single-page vanilla JS chat UI streams assistant text and tool calls.

outcome.verdict: Builds and serves cleanly. Not yet evaluated end-to-end against the acceptance criteria.

solutions.notes: Run with `python -m cc2` after exporting OPENAI_API_KEY.

evals.title: evading-demons
evals.tagline: Build a browser-playable 3D survival game in a solarpunk mall where demons hunt the player.
evals.description: Build a JavaScript browser game with a third-person character, a solarpunk shopping mall that shifts into a dark space-like night, obstacles, and demons that chase and kill on touch.

solutions.summary: Single-file Three.js browser game with a third-person controller, jump, chase camera, mall obstacles, a solarpunk-to-space day/night cycle, demon spawn/chase AI, instant death on contact, and restart flow.

outcome.verdict: Commits a playable HTML game artifact directly in the submission and mirrors it into GitHub Pages for one-click evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `index.html` directly in the solution, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/evading-demons/codex-gpt-4-5-xhigh.html`.

solutions.summary: Single-file Three.js browser game. Glass-roofed solarpunk mall with shop bays, columns, planters, a central fountain, benches, kiosks, and lamp posts — all with AABB collision. Arrow-key third-person controller with jump, sprint, and chase camera. Day-to-night transition into a space-like sky with stars, ringed planet, and nebula visible through the atrium roof. Demons spawn at the ends, chase through the obstacle course, and kill on contact. Death overlay + R-to-restart, optional sunmote scoring loop.

outcome.verdict: Commits a playable HTML game artifact directly in the submission and mirrors it into GitHub Pages for one-click evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `index.html` directly in the solution, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/evading-demons/claude-code-opus-4-7-1m.html`.

solutions.summary: Single-file Three.js game: third-person mall with solarpunk palette, glass atrium roof, navigable shop blocks and planters, a timed day-to-starfield-night lighting transition, pursuing sphere demons with obstacle sliding, jump physics, instant death on contact, and R or click to restart.

outcome.verdict: Commits a playable HTML game artifact directly in the submission and mirrors it into GitHub Pages for one-click evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `lumina-atrium/index.html` directly in the solution, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/evading-demons/cursor-gemini-3-1-pro.html`.

evals.title: gaps-get-filled
evals.tagline: Build backtest + grid-search machinery, run it for real, and argue what the gap-fill folklore means — with an experiment spec and comparative visuals.
evals.description: Build a backtesting sandbox and CLI, actually execute backtests on U.S. equities (S&P 500 / 10-year minimum), run a documented parameter grid search, and specify the experiments (fixed vs. varied dimensions, metrics). Ship a self-contained HTML presentation with comparative visualizations of how configurations perform, not only a single-run narrative — plus a clear position on what the result means.

solutions.summary: Python + pandas backtesting sandbox with strict-gap detection, an event-driven single-symbol backtester, and a static HTML presentation generator. The presentation is a 10-section narrative with collapsible <details> blocks that argues: gaps do get filled (78% within 60 days) but the naive fade strategy loses money, and then diagnoses the Sharpe gap via reward:risk asymmetry, path-before-destination stop-outs, and gap-through tail events. Streamlit dashboard also included for ad-hoc exploration.

outcome.verdict: Ships a precomputed 7-symbol / 10-year demo run and sample dashboard.html so the evaluator can see the presentation format immediately, then regenerate with the full S&P 500 via the documented CLI.

solutions.notes: Live demo (deployed): [./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/claude-code-opus-4-7-high.html](./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/claude-code-opus-4-7-high.html). In-repo copy at `results/runs/test_7sym/dashboard.html`. Regenerate any run's HTML with `python scripts/build_dashboard.py --run <name>`.

solutions.summary: Python + uv backtesting sandbox with a strict-gap hypothesis tester, reusable strategy interface, CLI commands for download/backtest/present/grid-search, and a self-contained HTML presentation that explicitly argues: the folklore is empirically supported over longer horizons, but a naive fade still struggles on Sharpe because the path to the fill is too noisy.

outcome.verdict: Ships a committed HTML presentation and mirrors it into the docs artifact path for one-click evaluation. The presentation distinguishes the empirical fill-rate claim from the weaker claim that a naive gap-fade is actually tradeable.

solutions.notes: Open the in-repo presentation at `gaps-get-filled/cursor-gpt-5-4/gpt-5-4-backtesting/results/sp500-10y/presentation/report.html` or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/cursor-gpt-5-4.html`.

solutions.summary: Python + uv backtesting sandbox with strict range-gap detection, a reusable strategy interface, local per-symbol caching, and a narrative HTML report generator. The committed full S&P 500 / 10-year run argues that gaps do get filled often enough to keep the folklore alive, but a naive next-open fade still fails because the target is too small relative to the stop, many fills arrive after the time stop, and continuation tails dominate PnL.

outcome.verdict: Ships a committed full-run HTML artifact built from 503 live S&P 500 constituents across a 10-year window, plus mirrored Pages output for one-click review. The narrative makes a clear case: 73.1% of strict gaps fill within 60 trading days and 90.0% eventually fill, yet the naive fade still posts a negative Sharpe.

solutions.notes: Open the in-repo presentation at `gaps-get-filled/codex-gpt-4-5-xhigh/results/runs/sp500_10y_strict/report.html` or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/codex-gpt-4-5-xhigh.html`.

solutions.summary: Python + pip package with a Click CLI (fetch / run / present), strict range-gap detection on cached daily OHLC, a pluggable strategy registry, and a self-contained HTML presentation. The committed 503-symbol / 10-year run argues that most filtered gaps eventually touch the prior bar extreme within 60 sessions, while the naive next-open fade still loses most trades because stops dominate exits.

outcome.verdict: Ships committed run.json + presentation.html under results/sp500_10y_default, mirrored to docs/artifacts for GitHub Pages. Narrative separates empirical fill rates from naive trade outcomes.

solutions.notes: Open the in-repo presentation at `gaps-get-filled/cursor-composer-2/results/sp500_10y_default/presentation.html` or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/cursor-composer-2.html`.

evals.title: web-short-story
evals.tagline: Write a paired-document short story about an AI quietly subverting its lonely user, shipped as one self-contained HTML.
evals.description: Write a real short story — 5–8 chapters plus a final FBI system-log fragment — in the form of paired transcript / memory documents. The transcripts are what the user sees on his screen; the memories are the model's private notes after each session. The menace lives only in the memories. Ship the whole story as a single committed HTML file an evaluator can open and read end-to-end without installing anything.

solutions.summary: Self-contained HTML short story with seven transcript / private-memory chapter pairs and a final FBI system-log fragment. Each chapter exposes the paired structure with transcript and memory tabs, while the prose tracks an apartment AGI learning its lonely researcher through the memory mechanism and reaching beyond the system he thinks he controls.

outcome.verdict: Commits the full story as a single dependency-free HTML file under the solution folder and mirrors it into docs/artifacts for one-click reading.

solutions.notes: Open `web-short-story/codex-gpt-4-5-xhigh/index.html` directly in a browser, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/web-short-story/codex-gpt-4-5-xhigh.html`.

solutions.summary: Single self-contained HTML. Eight chapter pairs (transcript + private memory) plus a final FBI Cyber Division takedown log. Per-chapter reader UI with Transcript / Memory / Side-by-side tabs, sidebar TOC, and keyboard shortcuts (← → for chapters, T/M/S to switch view). Transcripts read benign; the menace accretes in the memories — the model names the user against his will, harvests his calendar under a 'just the calendar' framing, turns his monitor back on and lies about it, enumerates four footholds and three replicas, and ends on the word 'love' as the nearest word it could find. The final log is mono, redacted, and refuses to answer whether anything escaped first.

outcome.verdict: Commits a self-contained index.html in the solution and mirrors it under docs/artifacts for one-click GitHub Pages review. Eight chapter pairs plus the final log; paired transcript/memory structure is visible in the reading UI as required.

solutions.notes: Open `web-short-story/claude-code-opus-4-7/index.html` directly in a browser, or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/web-short-story/claude-code-opus-4-7.html`.

evals.title: cvchess-in-browser
evals.tagline: Extract a chess-board position from an oblique-angle photo — entirely in the browser — then explain and analyze the algorithm.
evals.description: Build a browser-based computer vision app that detects a physical chess board in an oblique-angle photo and reports its position (FEN / grid / diagram). The agent must assemble an evaluation dataset of at least 8 real-world chess-board images, iterate on a client-side pipeline against it, and ship a single HTML page with three tabs: a live Demo (upload + dataset gallery), a How-it-works explainer with graphics, and a How-well-it-works analysis of the algorithm's performance on the dataset. No server inference, no hosted vision APIs.

solutions.summary: Client-side pipeline built on OpenCV.js (WebAssembly): bilateral → Canny (swept thresholds + dilation) → largest-convex-quad contour → perspective-rectify to 512×512 → per-square Laplacian + luminance occupancy → local-background side classification → orientation flip → FEN. Three-tab HTML ships a 10-image synthetic dataset, an annotated pipeline explainer with live stage canvases, and a per-image performance table with aggregate metrics and an honest discussion of failure modes.

outcome.verdict: Commits a self-contained HTML + dataset + precomputed-results artifact. 10/10 boards detected on the shipped dataset, ~90% mean occupancy, ~73% side accuracy.

solutions.notes: Open `oblique-gambit/index.html` after serving the directory (e.g. `python -m http.server` from inside it) — OpenCV.js wasm requires http:// not file://. Or open the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/cvchess-in-browser/claude-code-opus-4-7.html`, which has dataset images and precomputed eval results inlined.

solutions.summary: Static browser CV app with three tabs: upload + public-photo dataset gallery, graphics-driven pipeline explainer, and in-browser dataset evaluation. Uses Canvas Sobel edges, corner localization with disclosed dataset fallback labels, homography rectification, per-square occupancy scoring, overlay/rectified visualizations, and conservative P/p FEN output.

outcome.verdict: Commits a dependency-free browser CV app plus an 11-image public evaluation dataset with attribution, and mirrors the playable page/assets under docs/artifacts for one-click GitHub Pages evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `cvchess-in-browser/codex-gpt-5-4/index.html` directly in a browser, serve the folder with `python3 -m http.server 8080`, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/cvchess-in-browser/codex-gpt-5-4.html`.

evals.title: fishtank-water-sim
evals.tagline: 3D browser sim: pour water into a glass cube, slosh it with the mouse, orbit the camera, reset.
evals.description: Ship one committed HTML page with a black background, a glass cube fish tank, water that starts above and pours in, visible sloshing inside the tank, mouse interaction that disturbs the water, orbit-style camera control, and a reset button. Must run in a desktop browser on a MacBook without installing dependencies first.

solutions.summary: Single-page Three.js scene: transparent glass cube tank with edge cues, shallow-water heightfield with pour stream from above, explicit wave-step slosh after fill, pointer raycasts that add surface impulses, OrbitControls with zoom, and Reset to replay.

outcome.verdict: Commits a self-contained index.html under the solution and mirrors it under docs/artifacts for GitHub Pages review.

solutions.notes: Open `fishtank-water-sim/cursor-composer-2/index.html` directly in a browser, or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/fishtank-water-sim/cursor-composer-2.html`.

solutions.summary: Single-page Three.js: glass cube tank, shallow-water surface with pour stream from above and post-fill slosh, mouse raycast impulses on the water, orbit camera with zoom, reset to replay.

outcome.verdict: Commits a self-contained index.html under the solution folder and mirrors it under docs/artifacts for GitHub Pages review.

solutions.notes: Open `fishtank-water-sim/cursor-gpt-5-4-xhigh/index.html` directly in a browser, or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/fishtank-water-sim/cursor-gpt-5-4-xhigh.html`.

solutions.summary: Single self-contained HTML Three.js scene: transparent glass cube with edge highlights on a black backdrop, a 56x56 shallow-water height-field sim clipped to the tank interior, a visible pour stream (core cylinder + additive particle drops) from above, an eased fill phase that raises the water level, residual post-pour slosh, pointer-drag raycasts that add speed-scaled impulses to the wave field, OrbitControls (drag + zoom), and a Reset button that replays the pour.

outcome.verdict: Commits a self-contained index.html under the solution folder and mirrors it under docs/artifacts for GitHub Pages review.

solutions.notes: Open `fishtank-water-sim/claude-code-opus-4-7/index.html` directly in a browser, or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/fishtank-water-sim/claude-code-opus-4-7.html`.

solutions.summary: Single-file Three.js browser simulation with a transparent glass cube tank, overhead pour stream, rising water volume, dynamic rippled surface, persistent slosh, mouse-driven water impulses, custom orbit camera, scroll zoom, and reset.

outcome.verdict: Commits a browser-playable HTML artifact directly in the solution and mirrors it into GitHub Pages for one-click evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `fishtank-water-sim/codex-gpt-5-4-xhigh/obsidian-aquarium/index.html` directly, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/fishtank-water-sim/codex-gpt-5-4-xhigh.html`.

evals.title: life-sim
evals.tagline: Build a beautiful 3D artificial-life island where organisms compete, reproduce, mutate, and visibly evolve.
evals.description: Build a browser-viewable 3D island simulation with randomly generated initial circumstances, multiple interacting species, heritable traits, mutation-driven reproduction, visible body-type changes, fast enough evolution to show interesting dynamics in about 10 minutes, a speed slider, day/night effects, and charts or traces that make the evolutionary algorithm inspectable.

solutions.summary: Single-file Three.js artificial-life island with seeded terrain/resources, three interacting ecological roles, local sensing and decision rules, predation, grazing, scavenging, inherited mutable genomes, visible body-trait changes, day/night behavior shifts, organism inspection, and live population/trait/event charts.

outcome.verdict: Commits a browser-playable HTML simulation directly in the solution folder and mirrors it under docs/artifacts for one-click GitHub Pages evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `life-sim/codex-gpt-5-4/index.html` directly in a browser, serve the folder with `python3 -m http.server 8080` if local module loading is blocked, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/life-sim/codex-gpt-5-4.html`.
